title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia F1 Grand Prix cancellations to disadvantage Mercedes | ABC NEWS
description: The Formula One Grands Prix in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia have been cancelled amid the war in the Middle East.  The FIA Formula One World Championship’s organisers have decided not to select alternative events and shortened the season by two rounds.  PlanetF1 editor Matt Coch says the decision will likely disadvantage Mercedes and allow other teams to catch up on technical progress before the competition arrives in Europe in May. title: Danish King and Queen visit Uluru on Australia tour | ABC NEWS
description: King Frederik and Queen Mary of Denmark have visited Uluru in the first part of their visit to Australia. title: Denmark's Queen Mary and King Frederik X visit Uluru on Australia tour | ABC NEWS
description: The Danish royals have visited Uluru Kata Tjuṯa National Park in the Northern Territory, as King Frederik and Queen Mary begin Denmark's first state visit to Australia in 40 years. The trip from March 14 to 19 will include stops in the Northern Territory, Australian Capital Territory, Victoria and Tasmania, and is aimed at strengthening ties in the renewable energy, offshore wind, and energy-efficient urban development sectors. By Victoria Ellis for ABC News Northern Territory. title: ADF to assist with flood clean-up in Katherine, as other communities remain submerged | ABC NEWS
description: The Federal Minister for Emergency Management has confirmed the Australian Defence Force will be assisting with the flood clean-up in the Katherine region with around 50 members of the force on the ground from Monday. Meanwhile other communities in the Northern Territory remain flooded, with homes in the Daly River region still under water and a weather system in Central Australia under close-watch.
